title: Guarding against undated revisions
question: How should the tool stop a revision from being logged without a date, which hides the change from the Recent view?
rationale: A revision was once logged without a date, so Recent (which sorts by last activity, d7) fell back to the original decision date and the change never appeared in the 'Last 24 hours' bucket. Prevention is layered: SKILL.md and the Stop-hook backstop instruction both now require appending {from, reason, date} to history[], and generate.py prints a non-fatal warning when a decision's NEWEST history entry lacks a date. Keying the guard to only the newest entry catches the real failure mode on the next regenerate without nagging about old pre-timestamp entries (d2, d5) and without blocking a build or inventing dates for historical entries.

options.label: Non-fatal warning in generate.py, keyed to the newest history entry

tradeoffs.note: the exact case that broke (newest revision undated) is surfaced on the very next regenerate
never blocks a build, and doesn't nag about old pre-timestamp entries since only the newest entry is checked
a printed warning can be scrolled past; relies on the layered SKILL.md + hook instructions as the first line of defence

options.label: Hard-fail the build if any history entry lacks a date

tradeoffs.note: strongest guarantee — a missing date cannot ship at all
blocks regeneration, and would flag the old pre-timestamp entries (d2, d5) unless specially exempted

options.label: Doc-only — rely on SKILL.md and the hook instruction

tradeoffs.note: no mechanical net — which is exactly what already failed once when the hook instruction omitted the date
